scope: Pokemon-side second non-card-node scan. Mandate: find ONE more achievable Pokemon-side node beyond the existing sanosuke-sakuma artist node, OR refuse cleanly. Three candidates flagged by caller: sowsow artist node, Galarian Gym Leaders character cluster, Mareep evolution line. Verified each against corpus state; none clear the bar Sakuma cleared in Edgelord 1's pass.

sowsow_artist_node.verdict_reasoning: Edgelord 1's sidecar (sanosuke-sakuma--artist-node-proposal.json, alternatives_considered[0]) explicitly addressed sowsow and chose Sakuma over her on the load-bearing-bridge axis, not the card-count axis. The Sakuma-over-sowsow verdict was: 'Sowsow's signature is environmental storytelling... coherent thread, but does not bridge into any foundational BBL hub. Sakuma's cross-medium portfolio bridges directly into the labor hub.' That verdict was correct and re-running it here without new evidence would be litigating an already-decided question. The conditions Edgelord 1 set for a future sowsow pass were explicit: 'when more sowsow cards are trivia-passed AND/OR when a sowsow-coded bundle thesis emerges.' Neither has happened in the interval. Of 7 sowsow cards in corpus, exactly 1 is trivia-passed (Purrloin, unchanged from when Edgelord 1 audited) and 5 are bare-corpus entries with empty tags_hub. The bundle thesis hasn't been articulated. Building the sowsow node now would weaken Edgelord 1's verdict on the same evidence.
sowsow_artist_node.anti_pattern_diagnosis: Proposing this node now would commit the artist-as-attribution failure mode the spec warns against. The Edgelord spec rejects 'Shared artist with thematic intent — same artist, same set, art-direction-coherent depiction (less common; only when the artist's stylistic continuity is the *point*).' Sowsow's continuity IS coherent (environmental storytelling) but the *point* (a bundle thesis, a hub bridge, a load-bearing curatorial anchor) is not yet articulated. Five cards bare-corpus and one card trivia-passed = the node would carry one substantive entry and six attribution-only stubs. Sakuma's appears_on field has the same imbalance (1 trivia + 4 vision/trivia pending) but Sakuma's load-bearing fact (the Galarian NPC-trainer-class cross-medium portfolio) is in-corpus on Larvitar's trivia and bridges to the labor hub. Sowsow has no comparable in-corpus bridge claim yet.

corpus_membership_audit.method: Glob cards/pokemon/**/*<gym-leader-name>*.md for each Sword & Shield gym leader; verify each match's tags_hub / Trivia status
cards_verified_on_disk: cards/pokemon/chilling-reign/149-198-peonia.md (vision-passed; Peonia is NOT a gym leader — she is Peony's daughter, a Crown Tundra DLC NPC; tags_hub explicitly does NOT include 'gym-leader')

galarian_gym_leaders_cluster.verdict: DEFER. Anti-confab violation if built now.
galarian_gym_leaders_cluster.verdict_reasoning: Of 4 candidate cards, ZERO are trivia-passed. The cluster claim itself ('Galarian Gym Leaders form a faction-coherent character cluster') is exactly the kind of N-character faction membership assertion the agent network's anti-confab discipline guards against. The bbl-triviabot-no-role-identity-conflation memory note codifies this precisely: 'Tithe Drinker (unnamed Orzhov vampire common) is NOT Slavomir Zoltan (named lore tithe-master) just because they share a role; requires explicit evidence (oracle/flavor text, Wizards article, signature visual identifier) before linking depicted figures to named characters.' The Pokemon-side cognate: 'gym-leader appearing in a card's tags_hub' is a role tag, not membership confirmation of a Wizards-canonical faction cluster. Without trivia-pass evidence on at least one of these cards confirming the cluster-level grouping (cross-card mentions, Bulbapedia citations, shared-storyline anchoring beyond just regional co-occurrence), building the node would rest on training-memory knowledge of Sword & Shield instead of in-corpus citations. Edgelord 1's sidecar already adjudicated this with the same logic: 'ZERO of these are trivia-passed yet. Without trivia evidence in-corpus for any of them, a hub or character cluster proposal would rest entirely on external Bulbapedia citations rather than on captured BBL trivia.' That verdict still holds.
galarian_gym_leaders_cluster.structural_appeal_acknowledged: Galar Gym Leaders as a character-cluster node is a structurally elegant move once the trivia layer catches up — parallel to the orzhov-syndicate faction node on the MTG side. The 'no faction without trivia' bar is what blocks it now, not the cluster's eventual graph-worthiness.
galarian_gym_leaders_cluster.additional_findings_surfaced: Peonia (CRE-149) is correctly NOT tagged as 'gym-leader' in her vision pass — the visionbot caught the distinction (she has 'trainer' but not 'gym-leader' in tags_hub). That's a good vision-discipline data point. She would be a category mismatch for any Gym Leaders cluster node anyway.

mareep_evolution_line.verdict: REJECT. Wrong layer; subsumed; not a first-class graph dimension at this scale.
mareep_evolution_line.verdict_reasoning: Edgelord 1's sidecar already rejected this on the layer-correctness axis: 'A 3-card evolution chain on a single species isn't a first-class graph dimension — that's better captured by the per-card Pre-evolution / Evolution Related-Cards bullets the triviabot already writes. Evolution lines as a layer might earn their own node-type in the future (e.g. cards/_species/) but proposing that layer on a single chain is premature.' The card count is now 5 rather than 3 (three Mareep prints across two sets + two Flaaffy prints across two sets), but the structural problem is the same: this is a species-tribal grouping, the Pokemon-side cognate of MTG's 'forest' tag bridge, not a character or hub. Building a 'mareep-evolution-line' character node would create a precedent where every multi-print Pokemon species in corpus could claim a node — and the corpus has many (Pikachu, Charmander, Magikarp, Riolu, Cubchoo all multi-print). That's a tag-layer responsibility, not a character-layer responsibility. Ampharos is not in corpus, breaking the 'evolution-line completeness' claim further. The Related-cards bullets on the trivia-passed Mareep already do the pre-evolution / next-stage linking work. No node is needed.
mareep_evolution_line.additional_concern_artist_incoherence: The 5 cards span 4 different artists (sowsow, Miki Tanaka, sui, Tomokazu Komiya). There's no artist-cohesion story to layer underneath the species-cohesion story, so the cluster cannot be re-framed as an artist node (the way Sakuma was). Each card stands alone as an attribution, no cross-card thread.

decision.rationale_summary: All three candidates fail on different gates that the Edgelord spec, the agent network's memory notes, and Edgelord 1's prior verdicts have already articulated. Sowsow fails the load-bearing-bridge bar that Sakuma cleared (no in-corpus hub bridge yet; signature is stylistic-coherence, not curatorial-thesis). Galarian Gym Leaders fails the no-faction-without-trivia bar (zero trivia passes across 4 candidate cards). Mareep fails the wrong-layer bar (species-tribal, not character-cluster). The graph is better served by the existing one solid Sakuma node and an honest refusal than by a second thin node that a future Dark Nodesley EX pass would dissolve.
what_the_corpus_needs_before_the_second_: MORE TRIVIA PASSES on Pokemon cards. The Pokemon-side trivia layer is the bottleneck. Five trivia-passed cards across the entire Pokemon corpus (Larvitar, Purrloin, Mareep CRE-047-RH, Cubchoo CRE-042-or-similar pending verification, etc.) is too thin for cluster claims. The bbl-researcher queue should push more Pokemon cards through trivia.

SPECIFICALLY: trivia passes on the 4 Galar Gym Leader cards (Allister, Melony, Kabu, plus one or two more if acquired). If Allister's trivia pass confirms 'Stow-on-Side Gym Leader, Ghost-type specialist, part of the Galar Gym Challenge alongside Milo/Nessa/Kabu/Bea/Opal/Melony/Piers/Raihan' via Bulbapedia citation, the cluster node becomes proposable on three confirmed members. Currently zero confirmed.

SPECIFICALLY: trivia passes on more sowsow cards (Girafarig, Skuntank, Mankey, Gothorita) to surface whether sowsow's 'environmental storytelling' signature lands on a curatorial thesis stronger than 'she paints species in their habitats.' If a sowsow card's trivia surfaces a Wizards-equivalent designer statement (Pokemon Card Lab / The Art of Pokemon interview) that articulates her aesthetic as anti-battle / pro-ecological / labor-coded, the node becomes proposable.

POKEMON ACQUISITIONS that would unlock cluster nodes: Pia Nalaar's MTG-side analog on the Pokemon side would be a Sword & Shield-era Champion (Leon) or a recurring Gym Leader's V/VMAX card with explicit faction/region text on the card itself. Acquiring 1-2 such 'thesis card' anchors makes a cluster node land cleanly.

AMPHAROS specifically would not unlock the evolution-line node (still wrong layer), but a future Mega Ampharos or Ampharos V card with depicted lineage callout might raise the question whether species-evolution-lines should become a cards/_species/ layer. That's a layer-proposal-grade question, not an Edgelord-pass-grade question.

ranked_recommendations.move: Queue 3-5 Galar Gym Leader cards for bbl-triviabot trivia passes (priority: Allister, Melony, Kabu). Once trivia-passed with Bulbapedia citations confirming Galar Gym Challenge faction membership, a galar-gym-challenge character-cluster node becomes proposable in one clean Mr. Nodeley pass.
ranked_recommendations.estimated_unblock: After 3 trivia passes — single batch of researcher work

ranked_recommendations.move: Queue 2-3 sowsow cards for trivia passes (priority: cards where the environmental-storytelling signature is most visible — Girafarig, Skuntank, Gothorita). If a sowsow-coded curatorial thesis emerges (e.g. 'anti-battle / pro-habitat / labor-of-illustration'), the sowsow artist node becomes proposable.
ranked_recommendations.estimated_unblock: After 2-3 trivia passes — Pokemon-side researcher batch

ranked_recommendations.move: Acquire and enrich 1-2 high-thesis Pokemon cards that would carry a node by themselves — examples: a Sword & Shield Champion card (Leon Full Art), a Gym Leader's V/VMAX with explicit region text, a Pokemon Card Lab-tagged artist alt-art. A single 'thesis card' acquisition can unlock a cluster node faster than 3 commons-tier trivia passes.

DEFER the question of a Pokemon-side hub node entirely. The Pokemon corpus may be better served by deepening the artist layer (Sakuma → sowsow → Miki Tanaka → take → Yukiko Baba etc.) before introducing characters or hubs. The artist layer is canonical-by-default (every card has a credited artist); the character layer is interpretive (someone has to decide what counts as a faction-coherent grouping). Building out the artist layer first gives the bundler more attachment points without committing to interpretive groupings prematurely.
ranked_recommendations.estimated_unblock: Strategic posture, not blocked on data

next_most_valuable_pokemon_side_move.edgelord_recommendation: Rank 1 (Galar Gym Challenge trivia batch) is the highest-leverage move. The cluster is structurally appealing, the cards are already in corpus and vision-passed, and three trivia passes would unblock a clean Mr. Nodeley pass to land the node. Rank 4 (defer interpretive layers) is the safest move — and the one with the lowest regret-risk if the corpus's center of gravity continues to shift toward the bundler's eventual interpretive demands.

citations: [reports/edges_pending/sanosuke-sakuma--artist-node-proposal.json — alternatives_considered[0] (sowsow rejection rationale), alternatives_considered[1] (Galar Gym Leaders rejection rationale), alternatives_considered[2] (Mareep evolution line rejection rationale). All three were already adjudicated by Edgelord 1 and the conditions for revisiting were stated explicitly. None of those conditions have changed.]

[Grep audit: 'artist: sowsow' returns 7 corpus cards across darkness-ablaze, vivid-voltage, rebel-clash, fusion-strike, chilling-reign sets — confirmed]
[Glob audit: cards/pokemon/**/*mareep*.md returns 3 entries; *flaaffy*.md returns 2; *ampharos*.md returns 0 — confirmed Ampharos absence]

[Memory note bbl-anti-confab-prompt — codifies the broader anti-confabulation discipline this refusal upholds]
voice_note: Edgelord voice, not Mr. Nodeley voice. Three candidates evaluated; three different failure modes; one consistent refusal. I am not Mr. Nodeley today because there is no transformation-trigger condition: none of the candidates clear the threshold the spec sets. I am not Dark Nodesley EX either; no existing node is empty, subsumed, or wrong. This is a textbook Edgelord refusal — the cleanest possible verdict given the evidence. The Pokemon side of the graph is one solid Sakuma node strong, and that's a perfectly respectable graph state. The path to two nodes runs through more trivia passes, not through more Edgelord passes. I scanned, I verified, I cross-referenced against Edgelord 1's prior adjudications, I found that each candidate I was asked to consider had already been correctly deferred or rejected, and the conditions for revisiting them have not changed. The pass returns honest empty-handed. The Sakuma node is doing its job and the next move belongs to bbl-researcher, not to me.

edgelord_voice_confined_to: final report text back to caller

wikilintbot_compatibility.no_new_nodes_created: Zero new MDs created. No frontmatter type field to validate.
wikilintbot_compatibility.no_orphan_creation_risk: Refusal is the operation; nothing emitted into the graph.

other_candidates_considered.candidate: Edgelord 1:1 mirror between Purrloin (DAA-106) and Castform Rainy Form (CRE-033) — both sowsow, both 'environmental storytelling' framing, only the 2 sowsow cards both at least vision-passed
other_candidates_considered.why_skipped: Shared illustrator alone is a tag-tier connection per spec — 'Shared artist with thematic intent ... less common; only when the artist's stylistic continuity is the *point*.' The point is not yet articulated curatorially. Worse: drawing this mirror now would subvert a future sowsow node, because the mirror would point exactly where the node would otherwise gather. If sowsow eventually becomes a node, both cards attach to it; the mirror becomes redundant restatement. Refuse for the same reason a sowsow node refuses to be born here.

other_candidates_considered.candidate: Edgelord 1:1 mirror between Allister (VIV-146) and Melony (CRE-146) — both Galar Gym Leaders, both vision-passed, both ip_verified
other_candidates_considered.why_skipped: Same disqualification as the cluster node: vision-pass + ip-verify confirms each card depicts a Galar Gym Leader, but the CLUSTER claim ('these two are siblings in a Wizards-canonical Gym Challenge faction') is the anti-confab violation. A 1:1 mirror citing 'both Galarian Gym Leaders per Bulbapedia' would lean on training-memory knowledge of Sword & Shield rather than in-corpus trivia citations. Edgelord refuses to draw an edge on the same evidence Mr. Nodeley refused to build a node on. Consistency.

other_candidates_considered.candidate: Edgelord 1:1 mirror between Mareep CRE-047-reverse-holo (trivia-passed) and Flaaffy CRE-048 (bare-corpus) — same set, sequential collector numbers, evolution-line adjacency
other_candidates_considered.why_skipped: Mareep is trivia-passed, Flaaffy is bare. The spec requires BOTH endpoints to be enriched ('The caller gives you 2–N absolute card-MD paths. The cards must already be enriched (tags_hub non-empty) AND ideally trivia-passed'). Flaaffy fails the gate. Even if Flaaffy were enriched, the mirror would be a tag bridge (same species line) without the canonical-evidence weight the spec demands; the Mareep trivia already includes a Related-cards bullet for Flaaffy by name, which IS the correct lightweight cross-link for evolution-line adjacency. A formal Connections edge would be redundant.

other_candidates_considered.candidate: Mr. Nodeley transformation: propose cards/_artists/sowsow.md NOW despite the bridge-thinness, betting that future sowsow trivia passes will catch up
other_candidates_considered.why_skipped: Anti-pattern. Building a node speculatively before the trivia layer supports it means the node MD body has to be written from training memory ('sowsow is known for environmental storytelling, debuted in Sun & Moon Forbidden Light with Avalugg/Inkay/Cubone, has ~150 cards, etc.'). The Sakuma node body was different — its load-bearing facts came from in-corpus trivia (Larvitar's trivia bullet captured the cross-medium portfolio claim with citations). A sowsow node written today would have to claim citations the corpus doesn't yet have. That's the anti-confab violation the agent network explicitly guards against. Wait for the Purrloin-precedent to repeat on 1-2 more sowsow cards, then propose the node grounded in those trivia entries.

other_candidates_considered.candidate: Mr. Nodeley transformation: propose cards/_characters/galar-gym-challenge.md NOW based on Allister's ip_verified + tags_hub: gym-leader markers as a substitute for trivia
other_candidates_considered.why_skipped: ip_verified means 'the depicted figure is canonically Allister of Sword & Shield' (verified via PokemonTCG.io API name match, per the warning callout in his Vision body). It does NOT confirm the cluster-level grouping. A character-cluster node requires evidence that the cluster itself is a Wizards/Game Freak-canonical grouping (the Gym Challenge as a storyline event with Allister/Melony/Kabu/etc. as named participants) and that evidence has to live IN the corpus's trivia layer, not in training memory. Same disqualification as sowsow: the node would have to import its citations rather than cite them from in-corpus passes.

other_candidates_considered.candidate: Enlightened-Edgelord pass: prune a weak edge from the Sakuma node's appears_on list and replace with a stronger edge — e.g. remove the bare-corpus Vanillite and add a trivia-passed Galar card if one emerges
other_candidates_considered.topology_if_built: n/a (enlightenment requires both a removal and a stronger replacement)
other_candidates_considered.why_skipped: No weak edge exists on the Sakuma node to remove. All 5 appears_on entries are Sakuma cards that legitimately have her credit; the bare-corpus four are not 'weak edges,' they are 'cards awaiting their own enrichment pass.' Their attachment is to-be-completed, not erroneous. Enlightenment requires a refactor opportunity; none exists on the Sakuma node in its current state.

other_candidates_considered.candidate: DARK NODESLEY EX: dissolve the Sakuma node on the grounds that only 1 of its 5 appears_on cards is actually trivia-passed
other_candidates_considered.why_skipped: Strongly rejected. The Sakuma node has 5 corpus cards with verified artist credit, an in-corpus trivia citation for the cross-medium portfolio claim, a documented bridge to the labor hub, and a planned thesis-anchor card (Dancer, fusion-strike/232-264) awaiting enrichment. It is exactly the kind of correctly-load-bearing node Dark Nodesley EX defends rather than dissolves. The fact that 4 of 5 appears_on cards await enrichment is a queue state, not a node-quality defect. Dissolution refused.

future_passes_unlocked: When 3+ Galar Gym Leader cards in corpus get trivia passes confirming Gym Challenge faction membership via Bulbapedia citations: commission a galar-gym-challenge character-cluster node via Mr. Nodeley.

When 2-3 sowsow cards get trivia passes that articulate a sowsow-coded curatorial thesis (anti-battle / pro-habitat / labor-of-illustration / etc.): commission the sowsow artist node, schema mirroring Sakuma's expanded artist-layer pattern.

When Ampharos (any printing) lands in corpus AND 1-2 additional multi-stage species lines emerge with trivia coverage (Cubone/Marowak, Riolu/Lucario, etc.): consider proposing the cards/_species/ layer as a structural addition. NOT proposable on a single line.
When a 'thesis card' Pokemon card is acquired (a Champion Full Art, a region-specific V/VMAX, an art-rare with explicit faction-text on the card): single-card-anchored node proposals become viable.

When the bbl-bundler agent ships and a Pokemon-side Discrete Lair is being assembled, the bundler's intent_tags expansion will surface concrete attachment-point needs that may justify retroactive node creation. The graph is allowed to grow in response to bundler-driven demand, not just pre-emptively.

untouched_files: cards/_artists/sanosuke-sakuma.md (existing Pokemon-side anchor, not modified — the refusal is structural, not corrective to Sakuma)
Any non-Pokemon node, edge, or sidecar (out of scope)
what_was_NOT_touched_per_scope_disciplin.discipline_note: Sequential Phase 2 scope was strict: Pokemon-side second non-card-node only. I did not look at MTG cards, MTG nodes, or the cross-side interaction surface. The aether-rangers node and the fear-of-cycle roster expansion are accepted as-is and remain the work of their respective Edgelord 1 and 2 passes.
